qa_pairs.question: When was the Rorqual compute cluster fully unavailable for scheduled maintenance in September 2025, and what was the impact on its services?
qa_pairs.answer: The Rorqual compute cluster was fully unavailable for scheduled maintenance on September 3, 2025, from 8:00 a.m. (EDT) until 5:00 p.m. (EDT). During this time, all compute nodes were offline, jobs scheduled to complete after 8:00 a.m. remained in the queue, and associated network and storage systems were unavailable. The Narval cluster and cloud instances on the Béluga and Juno platforms were not affected by this outage.

qa_pairs.question: What was the duration and purpose of the extended outage for the Cedar cluster in June and July 2025, and how did it affect user access and services?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar cluster experienced a planned extended outage from June 16 to July 1, 2025 (originally scheduled until June 27), as part of infrastructure renewal activities to support critical data-centre power and cooling upgrades for a new system installation. During the outage, login node access was unavailable, and file systems were accessible read-only via Globus only (with access limited to the old project file system after June 23). Job submissions were suspended, though previously submitted jobs ran once the outage concluded. Cedar Cloud remained operational, despite brief interruptions, and a system reset was required for full recovery following an unexpected cooling tower failure.

qa_pairs.question: What electrical maintenance outages affected Béluga, Narval, and Juno (non-High Availability zone) in June 2025, and which services were impacted or remained operational during these events?
qa_pairs.answer: Two scheduled electrical maintenance outages affected Béluga and Narval compute nodes, and Juno Cloud instances in the non-High Availability zone during June 2025. The first was from June 6, 9:00 a.m. (EDT) to June 10, 12:00 p.m. (noon) (EDT), and the second occurred from June 13, 12:00 p.m. (noon) (EDT) to June 16, 12:00 p.m. (noon) (EDT). In both instances, compute nodes were shut down, and jobs were queued. For the June 6-10 outage, cloud instances on Béluga Cloud and in the Juno Cloud High Availability zone experienced short access outages, and storage systems on Béluga and Narval saw intermittent disruptions but remained accessible. For the June 13-16 outage, Béluga cloud instances and Juno cloud instances in the High Availability zone were not affected, and Béluga and Narval storage remained accessible via Globus and their respective login nodes.
